message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@divyanshu.sahu-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
What is the issue?
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
We're seeing a high rate of payment failures—about 40% of customer checkouts are not completing successfully right now.
@bob Can you check on the customers?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
We're getting a significant spike in customer complaints about failed payments, and support tickets have increased by about 3x in the last few minutes.
@bob What is the impact
Currently, around 40% of all payment attempts are failing, which is leading to a large number of incomplete orders and frustrated customers.
Which locations are down? or is it a global outage?
It looks like the failures are mostly concentrated in Europe, but I'm still seeing some successful payments from other regions.
@daniel any changes in the system recently?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Any updates on the investigation?
@Tinus we are checking this
Just to clarify, my PaymentService change was deployed 5 minutes ago but the new Bitcoin code isn't being called by the frontend yet, so I don't think it's related to the outage.
Europe is having outage
My CheckoutService change went live 15 minutes ago and mainly refactored the payment initiation logic, but I can provide logs or details if needed.
:star: Qualify the problem
Confirmed, payment failures in Europe are at 100% with zero successful transactions since the issue began.
Let me know if you want me to rollback either the AdService or PaymentDatabase changes deployed earlier today.
Looks like payment service was deployed 5 minutes ago
let's roll that back
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
My PaymentService change was deployed 5 minutes ago, but the new Bitcoin payment code isn't being invoked yet and all tests passed, so I don't think it's the cause.
My code isn't even being called yet, so I don't think rolling back is necessary.
Would make more sense to investigate Shay's change first?
My CheckoutService change was deployed 15 minutes ago and it refactored how payments are initiated, so it's worth checking if that's related to the issue.
Daniel has a point - let's try Shay's change first before touching the payment service
Sure, let's rollback CheckoutService
I will rollback my checkout change right away
Rollback complete!
@bob what's the impact now
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
We're still seeing a 100% payment failure rate in the UK, with no improvement after the CheckoutService rollback.
@Tanya lets rollback payments DB change
Alright, rolling back the PaymentDatabase change now
Rollback complete.
@shay I can see two deployments of checkout service one 15 minutes ago and another 20 minutes ago. Did we rollbacked both changes?
:star: Reviewed changes
Yes, both deployments have been rolled back so we're back to the previous stable version of CheckoutService.
@bob What is the update now?
any improvements?
:star: Manage actions and timings
There's still no improvement—UK payments continue to fail at 100% even after both rollbacks.
No improvements so far—UK payment failures remain at 100% after all recent rollbacks.
Let's rollback PaymentsService
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
There might be the bug in your code Daniel
I understand the concern, but my code passed all unit tests and isn't supposed to affect current payment flows—can we check the logs first before rolling back?
I did notice that Daniel's change included modifications to some shared libraries used specifically in the UK payment flows, which might be worth a closer look.
Just rollback first
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
we can check the logs later
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
@bob keep an eye on this
Would you like me to try placing a test order to confirm if payments are working now?
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
@Tanya @Shay @Daniel keep the eye on the system and confrm is everyrthing are back to normal
I'll check the payment service logs now.
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
:bulb: keep the business informed regularly
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day
Great work everyone - crisis averted!
